questions.question: White light passes through a simple triangular glass prism and exits as a fan of colors. The root cause is that…
options: The glass absorbs some colors completely
The prism's base reflects part of the light
The light source flickers between colors
The refractive index of glass varies with wavelength, so blue light bends more than red
questions.explanation: Dispersion means each wavelength sees a slightly different index, so each color refracts through a slightly different angle and the beam fans into a spectrum.

questions.question: An achromatic doublet combines a crown-glass element with a flint-glass element in order to…
options: Double the magnification
Increase light transmission
Cancel chromatic aberration by setting the two glasses' dispersions against each other
Make the lens shatterproof
questions.explanation: The converging crown element and diverging flint element have opposing dispersions, so the color-focus errors largely cancel and the fringing of a single-element lens disappears.

questions.question: For total internal reflection to guide light along an optical fiber, the cladding glass must…
options: Have a higher refractive index than the core
Have a lower refractive index than the core
Be fully opaque
Be metallic
questions.explanation: TIR only occurs when light travels from a denser medium toward a less dense one beyond the critical angle, so the core must be the higher-index glass and the cladding the lower.

questions.question: A new rigid borescope produces a much brighter, sharper image than an older scope of the same diameter and length. The most likely design reason is…
options: Hopkins rod-lens relays replacing thin lenses separated by long air gaps
A coherent fiber bundle in place of lenses
A digital zoom function
A larger eyepiece lens
questions.explanation: Rod-lens relays fill the tube with glass and allow larger optical apertures in the same diameter, transmitting several times more light with better resolution and contrast.

questions.question: Heavy edge enhancement is active during a video examination. A Level II should recognize that this processing can…
options: Create halo artifacts that mimic or mask real edge detail at an indication
Permanently repair broken image fibers
Increase the true optical resolution of the lens
Eliminate the need for white balance
questions.explanation: Enhancement manipulates pixel values, not optics — it can sharpen the look of the image while drawing artificial halos or smoothing away real fine detail, so borderline indications should also be viewed with it off.

questions.question: You must inspect a combustion chamber through a 10 mm spark-plug port; the area of interest is 80 mm inside and offset around a corner. The minimum suitable instrument is…
options: A rigid borescope with forward view
An inspection mirror and flashlight
A flexible scope — fiberscope or videoprobe — with an articulating tip
A 10× magnifier
questions.explanation: Access is through a small port with the target offset around a bend, so a rigid scope's straight line of sight cannot reach it — flexibility and tip articulation are required.

questions.question: Magnification of the image seen through any borescope is best described as…
options: Fixed by the eyepiece marking
Increasing as the tip-to-target distance decreases
Constant across the entire field of view
Equal to 250 divided by the scope length
questions.explanation: Apparent size depends strongly on standoff — closer looks bigger — which is exactly why sizing through a scope requires a reference or a calibrated measuring system.

questions.question: Two parallel laser beams spaced a known 50 mm apart are projected onto a subsea weld in the camera's view. Their purpose is to…
options: Illuminate the weld for the camera
Detect magnetic flux leakage
Provide a known dimensional scale in every image
Measure water temperature at depth
questions.explanation: Because the beams are parallel, their dots stay 50 mm apart on the target regardless of camera distance, putting a trustworthy scale reference into each frame.

questions.question: A pipe crawler camera system is preferred over a push-tube camera when…
options: The line is only 50 mm in diameter
Only the first meter of the line needs examination
The camera must be located from the surface using a sonde
The line is large-diameter and long, requiring powered travel, high-output lighting, and pan-tilt-zoom viewing
questions.explanation: Crawlers are motorized tractors for roughly 150 mm and larger lines, carrying PTZ cameras and lights over long distances on a tether — beyond what hand-pushed rod systems can do.

questions.question: A specification requires visually verifying the anchor profile of blast-cleaned steel before coating. The standard VT aid is…
options: A surface (profile) comparator held beside the surface
A stereo measurement tip
A macro-etchant
A polarizing filter
questions.explanation: Surface comparators are calibrated reference standards judged by eye and touch against the actual surface — the recognized way to verify texture and anchor profile visually.
